title: cyclamen coum subsp. elegans
intro: Cyclamen coum subsp. elegans, also known as the elegant Persian cyclamen, is a perennial plant species belonging to the primrose family (Primulaceae). Traditionally, the roots and tubers of Cyclamen coum subsp. elegans have been used in herbal medicine, valued for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and expectorant properties. The plant's roots and tubers are believed to offer health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, Cyclamen coum subsp. elegans is often dried and used in teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although caution is advised due to potential toxicity.
